mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in consumer spending capacity, triggering a negative repricing of cyclical equities over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, depressing equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO and RSAFS decline concurrently, with predictive power vanishing in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, negatively impacting financials and consumer discretionary stocks over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline but SPX forward returns remain positive, indicating decoupling between real estate and broader equity markets.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises by more than 0.4 percent month-over-month, it signals entrenched inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPI spikes occur but equity valuations expand due to strong earnings growth overriding discount rate effects.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with accelerating consumer credit balances indicates an overheating economy that historically precedes aggressive monetary tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, or equity markets rally on strong growth expectations despite tight credit conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent sustains corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of valuation levels.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index below 98.5 signals supply chain bottlenecks or weakening manufacturing demand, triggering a negative repricing of industrial and materials equities over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below threshold but SPX forward returns remain positive due to service sector strength offsetting manufacturing weakness.
